entry.title: Re: Override back button on FLP
entry.content:
My attempts to capture the browser back event have never been completely successful in the past. 

If you're using the standard draft-handling mechanism, then the user entries should be saved as draft and they can continue later when they enter the object page again. Not sure if that behaviour is acceptable for your use case.

entry.title: SAP Manufacturing Execution training
entry.content:
Hi

 

The VARNISH step only is used if there are scratches. If the MECA part is scratch-free, it goes directly to the CAB step. If the MECA part has scratches it goes to VARNISH step.

I integrated a data collection by selecting the Boolean type in the MECA operation. On the false value I entered “YES” (application of varnish) and the true value “NO”. I assigned it a NC_CODE “DEFAUT_ASPECT”

I tried to attach nonconformance scripts to any branch but if I confirm “yes” or “no”, the system sends same CAB operation (VARNISH & CAB)

How do I ensure that when I validate “YES” it goes to the VARNISH step and “NO” to the CAB step

Thank you in advance for your help

Best regards

entry.title: Create button is Greyed-out in Manage incidents > People Tab for creating Business Partner
entry.content:

I am encountering an issue with the "Manage Incidents" app. When I try to add an injured person in the "People" tab, the "Create" button is greyed out and I am unable to proceed with adding the injured person details. This issue arises even when I add the injured person at the time of reporting the incident using the "Report Incident" app.

Has anyone else faced this issue? How can I activate the "Create" button in the "People" tab to add an injured person? Any guidance or suggestions would be greatly appreciated.

Added Screenshot For you reference

 

SAP Environment, Health, and Safety Management , SAP S/4HANA business partner SAP EHS Safety Issue  PLM (Product Lifecycle Management)  #

Thank you!

Hello,

Original question: I could read in the article that it is possible to remove tag mappings with competencies via Integration Center. Can you please let me know how to do it?

I am sorry, I found it: The import overwrites the assigned tags. If you want no assigned tags, you just put it empty.

Martin Pösinger

entry.title: Re: Authority Check-For Cost Center Based on User name
entry.content:
Hello 

First of all, you should check the cost center is reachable at this exit.  If yes, then you must fill lv_cs by cost center  that pass to exit.

SELECT count(*)
  FROM z_cs_auth
  WHERE uname = @SY-uname AND kostl = @iv_kostl."iv_kostl is cost center that reachabel form exit.
IF sy-subrc is not INITIAL.
  " No entries found for the user in the custom table
  MESSAGE e001(zuser_auth).
  EXIT.
ENDIF.

Marco

entry.title: SAP Commerce Cloud Explained: A Video Guide for Business Users
entry.content:
Abstract

Whether you're a stakeholder, manager, consultant, or business user of SAP Commerce Cloud, you will need clear, concise, and insightful information. To help you navigate the extensive functionalities of SAP Commerce Cloud, the Commerce education/training team curated a series of videos that cover every module you might need. All these videos are inside the Business End User SAP Commerce Cloud channel. This blog post serves as your ultimate guide and index to these invaluable resources. Get ready to transform your e-commerce capabilities and drive business success!

This blog post can also serve as a foundational self-study resource to enhance your preparation for the SAP Commerce Cloud Business User Certification exam. It covers most of the pertinent topics and provides a first-level introduction to them. However, for a more comprehensive learning experience, I strongly recommend enrolling in the SAP Commerce Cloud Business User C4H320 training. This program offers direct instruction from SAP-authorized instructors who will guide and support you throughout your learning journey.

(Please be aware that some videos referenced in this guide may display user interfaces (UIs) or storefronts with minor differences compared to the latest version of SAP Commerce Cloud. Due to the rapid pace of monthly updates, it can be challenging to ensure all videos reflect the most current version. However, the features and functionalities should remain consistent, and you can safely disregard the UI or storefront differences.)

Overview

First, please watch the video titled:

Introduction to SAP Commerce Cloud

to get a brief overview of the basics of SAP’s powerful e-commerce solution.

As explained in the video, SAP Commerce Cloud is composed of a number of key modules. The most essential ones are illustrated in the following diagram:

(From Bottom to Top)

  • Platform - The Platform module serves as the foundational layer providing core functionalities such as user management, security, scalability, and cloud deployment. It is designed to support customizable and scalable e-commerce solutions.
  • Product Content Management (PCM) - PCM is a critical module for managing product information across all sales channels. It allows businesses to centralize product data, ensuring consistency, accuracy, and enrichment of product details.
  • Commerce (Promotions & Coupons, Search, etc.) - This module focuses on key commerce functionalities such as Promotions & Coupons, Search & Navigation, Adaptive Search, etc.
  • Customer Experience Management (aka. Web Content Management) - this module helps manage and deliver digital content to various customer touchpoints. It includes tools for content creation, personalization, and management, all aimed at enhancing user experiences.
  • Order Management - This module handles the full lifecycle of customer orders. This includes order capture, processing, fulfillment, and returns management.
  • Customer Support - This module empowers customer service teams with tools to manage customer inquiries, complaints, and service requests effectively.
  • B2B - Specifically designed for Business-to-Business commerce scenarios, the B2B module extends the functionalities of all the other modules and includes B2B driven features such as account management, self-service organizational structures, replenishment ordering, customized pricing, and approval workflows.
  • UIs (Storefronts, Backoffice, SmartEdit) - refers to all the user interfaces employed for interacting with various types of data of all the underlying modules.

For a quick overview of the Platform module, feel free to watch the Platform & Extensibility video.

Following that, we will delve into each of the other modules, introducing: 1. Backoffice UIs, the primary tools of business users; 2. PCM for product data management; 3. Customer Experience Management to present the e-commerce shop with product data; 4. Commerce for promotions, coupons and search to enhance the e-commerce shop; 5. Order Management & Customer Support for order fulfillment and the related customer support features; 6. B2B for a quick overview of B2B features.

1. Backoffice UIs

Among the various graphical user interfaces available out-of-the-box in SAP Commerce Cloud, Backoffice is undoubtedly the most frequently used by business users.

Backoffice offers several standard perspectives (aka. cockpits) tailored to different user roles and feature sets. To get an overview of these perspectives, you can watch the video:

Backoffice Perspectives

One notable perspective is the Backoffice Administration cockpit. This perspective is versatile and can be used to manage various types of data. Let's take a closer look at how to manage system data within the Backoffice Administration Cockpit:

Managing System Data in Backoffice Administration Cockpit

In addition, Backoffice includes many useful general features as well, such as:

Deep Linking in Backoffice

For the other use of Backoffice (e.g., Product Cockpit, Adaptive Search Cockpit, etc.), please refer to the corresponding Backoffice perspectives in the modules listed below.

2. Product Content Management (PCM)

For business users, a typical first task within the context of Product Content Management (PCM) in SAP Commerce Cloud is to become familiar with the Backoffice Product Cockpit. To get started quickly, you can watch an introductory video:

Backoffice Product Cockpit

Now, let's delve into the specific steps required to perform essential PCM tasks in Backoffice using the Product Cockpit:

  1. Create a Product Category
  2. Add a Product to a Category
  3. Insert New Products Using Excel
  4. Filter the Product Catalog Using the Welcome Dashboard
  5. Approve a Product Using the Welcome Dashboard
  6. Compare Products in the Backoffice Product Cockpit
  7. Bulk Approve and Bulk Edit Products
  8. Bulk Edit Products Using Excel Export / Import
  9. Synchronize a Product or Catalog  

Additionally, media management is a crucial aspect of Product Content Management. To learn how to add media to products within the Product Cockpit, watch the following instructional videos:

  1. Add Media to a Product
  2. Uploading Media to Backoffice Product Cockpit Using Excel

After watching the videos, you should now have a solid understanding of how to manage basic product data in SAP Commerce Cloud. In addition to these fundamentals, SAP Commerce Cloud offers several standard product modeling approaches to extend the basic product model. For an overview, please watch this video:

Product Modeling (Overview)

For a more in-depth look into specific product modeling approaches, consider exploring the following resources:

  • Identifying the Basic Product Variants
  • An Introduction to Configurable Products
  • Introducing New Features for Product Configuration

Lastly, it's important to note that for product content management in the Backoffice, the workflow management support in the collaboration center is an invaluable tool. For more information, please watch the corresponding video:

Working with Workflows

Please note that workflows can also be used to collaboratively manage data from other modules. You will see examples of this later.

3. Customer Experience Management (aka. Web Content Management)

After preparing the product data, we'll need to present it on an e-commerce shop. This is achieved using a Web Content Management System (WCMS) in the Customer Experience Management module. Check out this video for an overview of WCMS and to understand how backend data integrates with the frontend:

An overview of Web Content Management System

The standard frontend solution in SAP Commerce Cloud is known as Composable Storefront. Watch this video for a quick introduction:

Introducing SAP Commerce Cloud Composable Storefront

For easy and intuitive management of frontends and related web content, SmartEdit is provided. Start with this video, Experience and Web Content Management in SmartEdit for a quick introduction to SmartEdit. Afterward, you can delve into the following videos for more detailed information on working with SmartEdit:

  • Create a Static Page
  • Add a Component to a Page
  • Add a Component to a Shared Content Slot
  • Edit a Category Page
  • Creating User-Friendly URLs with SmartEdit
  • Clone a Page
  • Remove, Delete and Restore a Page
  • Content Versioning in SmartEdit
  • Extending your Website Structure with Navigation Management
  • Add a New Navigation Node

In addition, Personalization is a crucial aspect of customer experience management. To see how to add personalization to an existing page, you can watch the video "Add a Personalization to a Page." This feature is available in SmartEdit's personalization mode. For a brief introduction to this mode, view "Personalizing Your Commerce Storefront in SmartEdit".

Finally, as mentioned earlier, workflows can be used for collaboration work within SAP Commerce Cloud. To understand how to effectively use workflows in SmartEdit, you can watch the video Working with Workflows in SmartEdit.

4. Commerce (Module)

Now that our e-commerce shop is set up with product data, it's time to enhance it using the Commerce module.

1. Among the many features provided in this module, promotions are definitely the first ones to explore. For a quick overview, check out this video: Working with Promotion Engine in Backoffice (Overview).

Once you've watched the video, you can delve deeper into the specifics of working with promotions in Backoffice through the following topics:

  • Creating a Promotion Rule Based on a Template
  • Creating a New Promotion Rule
  • Examining a Promotion Rule in Backoffice

You can also personalize promotions through Commerce Customization. For a quick demonstration, check out the Create a Commerce Customization video.

2. The next significant topic is the creation and management of coupons. You can watch the following videos to learn how to create coupons and apply them with promotions:

  • Creating a Single-Code Coupon
  • Creating a Multi-Code Coupon
  • Creating a Coupon-Based Promotion

Additionally, there are special kinds of coupons designed for specific business requirements. For a quick introduction to an advanced coupon example, you can watch the Customer Coupons video.

3. To further enhance user experience, optimizing search functionalities is crucial. While the Search & Navigation module can be configured to enhance search capabilities, it requires a more technical approach. On the other hand, Adaptive Search can be utilized by business users to manage, optimize, and create more meaningful search experiences. For a quick summary, refer to the Overview of Adaptive Search Perspective.

For detailed guidance on actions you can perform within the adaptive search cockpit in Backoffice, please watch the following tutorials:

  • Boosting a Single Item
  • Boost Multiple Items via Boost Rules
  • Adding a New Facet to the Storefront
  • Promoting a Facet in Adaptive Search
  • Excluding a Facet in Adaptive Search

Lastly, understanding the basics of Merging Search Profiles is essential. This knowledge will help you unify and refine search configurations for an optimal user experience.

5. Order Management & Customer Support

After a customer visits an e-commerce shop, searches for their favorite products, and adds them to the shopping cart alongside any applicable promotions or coupons, they place an order to initiate the order fulfillment process. To understand the entire lifecycle from order capture to fulfillment, please watch the "Fulfilling Orders" video.

In addition, customer support is crucial to ensuring customer satisfaction before, during, and even after the shopping process. Please watch this video:

Improving your Customer Service with Backoffice Customer Support (Overview)

for an overview of the customer support features.

As you see, customer support is provided through two graphical user interfaces: the Customer Support Cockpit in Backoffice and the Assisted Service Mode (ASM).

You can perform a variety of customer support activities in the Customer Support Cockpit in Backoffice, such as: Creating a New Customer Account in the Backoffice Customer Support Cockpit. However, ASM offers a more modern and intuitive approach, making it particularly appealing for business users. Please watch the video:

Improving the User Experience with the ASM (Overview)

to get an overview of ASM, and review the following videos to learn some essential actions you can perform with ASM:

  • Creating a New Customer via ASM
  • Access Customer Data in ASM
  • Give or Withdraw Consent on a Customer's Behalf in ASM
  • How to Place an Order for a Calling Customer (ASM)

6. B2B

The Business-to-Business (B2B) commerce module of SAP Commerce Cloud is specifically designed to support and enhance the complexities and intricacies involved in B2B commerce transactions. These could include bulk orders, complex pricing models, order approval processes, or highly specific delivery requirements unique to B2B interactions. You can check the webcast recording of

Leveraging SAP Commerce Cloud for B2B Customers

to get a detailed overview of the B2B commerce module.

You may also find it useful to watch the following videos:

  • Replenishment and Scheduled Orders (B2B)
  • Approving Orders (B2B)

These provide basic demo examples of standard B2B support.

Last but not least

I hope this structured guide, complete with references to our carefully curated video content, provides you with a concise overview of the immense potential of SAP Commerce Cloud.

Don't forget to subscribe to the channel of Business End User SAP Commerce Cloud for the latest updates:

or leave your comments or questions here.

Happy learning!

entry.title: Partial scrap confirmation in DM, Quantity Confirmation, WC POD
entry.content:
Dear DM Experts,

We have a customer requirement to have a similar quantity confirmation possiblity for the discrete manufacturing in DM like we already have in S/4HANA. 

The question is how can I do a partial scrap confirmation in DM? What are the procedure, where I can say I have a defect and from total 5 product I can produce only 4. In S/4HANA I can simply type in yield quantity 4 and scrap 1. 

However in DM I can scrap the whole SFC only. Or I need to split the SFC first to 1 piece and 4 pieces and scrap the SFC belongs to the product, which has defect. (or I am missing something and I could type in somewhere, scrap = 1)

On the Order POD there is a Plugin "Quantity Confirmation", which seems to be exactly what we would like to have for a Work Center POD. Does anybody know, why this Plugin isn't available for the Work Center POD?

What is the best way to handle the above mentioned simple scenario, where there are all together 5 products, and during the production 1 piece must be marked as scrap from the 5? Is that only possible using the splitting functionality in DM or is there a better way?

Thank you for the help in advance.

Regards,
Oliver

entry.title: Re: SAP BO 4.2 SP05: Windows Server OS Upgrade From 2008...
entry.content:
Hi.

You can't upgrade directly from 2008 to 2016; you would need to upgrade to 2012 first and then from there to 2016.

With that said, doing an in-place OS upgrade of a BO server is not recommended.  See this note and this one.  The recommended solution is to build a new server, then do a fresh install of the OS and the BO software.  

With that said, BI4.2 is now out of support, you should consider BI4.3 if you can.

You would need to create an app that shows the CVE's being used by CR API's and how it could be affected in the app.

The only way to get a support contract now is to purchase Crystal Reports Server, you get a 1 year contract with it.

It's about $1000, but also can add a huge amount of functionality to your project, note you will need to convert how you open reports, relatively easy to do.

More info here:

https://www.sap.com/products/technology-platform/crystal-server.html

entry.title: SAP Digital Manufacturing - subscribe to MQTT topics
entry.content:
Hello,

I would like to subscribe to a MQTT topic in SAP Digital Manufacturing. The MQTT broker runs on the same machine as the SAP Production Connector. The process is as follows: A OPC UA server publishes events and alarms which are consumed by a Node-RED flow which further publishes the message as JSON to a Mosquitto MQTT broker and topic.

The problem now is that I am not able to connect to the MQTT broker from SAP Digital Manufacturing via Production Connector because I would have to upload a "Client Certificate" consisting of a private key, client certificate, CA certificate and root certificate in the .pem format according to SAP Help Portal. I've done that and also integrated the client key and all certificates into one .pem file. After uploading the certificate chain as single file into SAP Digital Manufacturing in the app "Manage Message Brokers", Section 3rd Party Brokers and Production Connectors, I receive the following error:

Couldn't upload Production Connector client certificate. Response: An error occurred while processing your request.

Any ideas how to further proceed here?

Thank you in advance for your help!

Best regards
Fabian

entry.title: A Magical First
entry.content:
There is magic in first times. The novelty of those moments makes up for most of our core memories. Most people have vivid memories of their first day in school or their first/childhood friend, their first job and their first day at that job or their first pet, among others… These are moments outside the wheel of repetitive days. Alexandra Hofmann, a cloud solution architect at SAP’s Corporate Processes and Information Technology (CPIT), is no stranger to firsts: Despite the scale of the global juggernaut that SAP Sapphire is, it was an ocean that Alexandra was comfortable sailing in; she had her first presentation at SAP Sapphire in Orlando, in June 2024. 

Alexandra Hofmann

“My first time presenting at SAP Sapphire was a fantastic opportunity to experience how and what SAP communicates to our customers and partners, and how all products and topics are linked. For me, it was a great learning opportunity to present in front of many people, not only for my career but also for my personal development,” says Alexandra. 

Alexandra started at SAP in 2013 as part of SAP’s dual studies program. Studying internal business administration and information technology and working in different departments in her practical phases prepared her well for her start within the Platform Center of Excellence team in 2016. Since then, Alexandra has been an invaluable part of the team which defines SAP’s internal IT strategy for cloud development within CPIT, starting off as a developer on SAP Business Technology Platform for the Neo environment, conducting HTML5 & Java trainings, designing the architecture of the customer data hub tool, and now focusing on enabling CPIT colleagues through SAP IT cloud developer enablement programs.  

Shortly after the SAP Sapphire event, another first came knocking on her door: Alexandra has since delivered two expert sessions on the Inside SAP YouTube series. In Alexandra’s first session she explained how we run SAP Business Technology Platform at scale, utilizing AI capabilities and agile methodologies to optimize operations, increase developer efficiency, and accelerate innovation as a large-scale enterprise. In the ‘Discover How SAP Accelerates Business Using SAP Build Solutions’ session, viewers experienced how SAP’s internal IT team builds apps without writing code, using AI-driven code-development support, automating workflow processes, and designing central business sites unleashing the power of SAP Build solutions. 

Alexandra Hofmann delivering a presentation at SAP Sapphire in Orlando in June 2024.

“Holding the presentation at SAP Sapphire in front of customers and partners was the best way to prepare for the YouTube sessions as the direct feedback afterwards showed how important SAP runs SAP stories are,” says Alexandra. 

“Our unit and our SAP runs SAP scenarios serve as frontrunners for our customers on using SAP Business Technology Platform. This is based on our experiences and learnings. Customers can leverage our lessons and apply them to their own cloud setup,” she adds. 

Her webcast was preceded by the opening session with Oliver Gutzeit and Christian Freytag’s session on SAP’s cloud business journey’s impact on our Enterprise Architecture. The upcoming sessions will serve interesting insights into AI, about our AI offerings in SAP IT and how we infuse our core business processes with AI, for example, in our HR (Human Resource) area.  

“I would definitely do it again because, in my opinion, we as well as our customers and partners can learn a lot from each other, and this offers a platform for great exchange opportunities,” says Alexandra. 

 Indeed, a first to remember!

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408: Final What's New & Product Assistance available
entry.content:
With immediate effect, the final What's New information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 is now available. SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is based on the technical product SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

To access the Final What’s New information, please use the What’s New Viewer.

For further formats and languages of the What's New information, as well as pre-filtered views, please use the following SAP S/4HANA Cloud Help Portal page.

In addition, the final Product Assistance (aka Application Help) for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 is available. Please use the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Help Portal page to access the Product Assistance in multiple languages.

For you as a customers, we recommend to also use the Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool.

The Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool provides you with a personalized and tailored view on What’s New information based on the activated and used scope within your system. The information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 will be available in RASD within the next few days. A valid Customer S-user ID is required for login. We recommend to use RASD to prepare for upgrades and updates of your system and to focus on the new and changed features that are relevant for you as a customer.

There is a new commenting feature available for the What’s New in the release SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2408. The feature is now available for this release as part of a pilot that will end on August 21, 2024. During the pilot, authors will evaluate your feedback and incorporate it into the documentation, if needed. We hope that by opening this channel of communication, we can learn how to further improve our What’s New content for you. 

To use this feature, you need to be logged into the Help Portal. The feature will only be available in the English version of the documents. To learn more about how the feedback process works in SAP Help Portal, see Giving Documentation Feedback.

entry.title: Re: How to communicate the value of Enterprise Architecture in SAP projects
entry.content:
Hi @BillyGee 

I feel the same and this very much reminds me of the metaphor used by Gregor Hohpe in "The Software Architect Elevator" (brilliant read if you haven't read that yet) of how each organisation has their own "map" of how the "world" looks like and what is in the center of said "map". So of course most SAP SIs place SAP in the center, which might not work well in all instances with the customers own "map" in mind.

And in the same way I think industry-standards can more often than not be more beneficial than proprietary frameworks / accelerators that each SI brings to the table.

Best,

Toni

entry.title: Runtime Exception ITAB_DUPLICATE_KEY behaves differently for CORRESPONDING and FOR loop
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I think this is a shortcoming from SAPs side, the runtime exception ITAB_DUPLICATE_KEY behaves differently in case of CORRESPONDING component operator and FOR loop. In case of the former, the runtime exception cannot be caught. However, in case of the later there is an exception class CX_SY_ITAB_DUPLICATE_KEY which can be caught and handled. 

Scenario: Moving data from an internal table of type standard to internal table of type hashed.

Consider the following code.

 

TYPES:
  BEGIN OF _structure,
    id   TYPE i,
    name TYPE string,
  END OF _structure,

_standard_table_type TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF _structure WITH EMPTY KEY,
_hashed_table_type   TYPE HASHED TABLE OF _structure WITH UNIQUE KEY id.

DATA(itab1) = VALUE _standard_table_type( 
                ( id = 1 name = |Tamit| ) 
                ( id = 1 name = |Tamit| ) 
                ( id = 2 name = |tamit| ) 
                ( id = 3 name = |Tamit New| ) ).

 

Now if we create a hashed internal table from the above mentioned standard table using for the exception can be caught as displayed below.

 

TRY.
  DATA(itab2) = VALUE _hashed_table_type( FOR <itab> IN itab1 ( <itab> ) ).
    CATCH cx_sy_itab_duplicate_key INTO DATA(exception1).
      out->write( |Exception raised via For Loop| ).
      out->write( exception1->get_text( ) ).
      out->write( exception1->get_longtext( ) ).
ENDTRY.

 

Also, we can create the same hashed table using corresponding - component operator as shown below. But in this case the exception cannot be caught.

 

DATA(itab3) = CORRESPONDING _hashed_table_type( itab1 ).

 

 The question to the community is that shouldn't in both the case the exception be allowed to be caught as the runtime error is same in both the cases.

SAP NetWeaver Application Server for ABAP SAP S/4HANA Cloud ABAP Environment ABAP Extensibility

entry.title: Finance in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408
entry.content:
Hello everyone, Uli here from SAP's Product Engineering Finance Team. I'm thrilled to share an update on our latest release - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 for Line-of-Business Finance. This update highlights significant innovations including business AI:

  • Financial Planning and Analysis: Cost Center Review Booklet
  • Financial Planning and Analysis: Review Booklet for Cost Centers: Gaining Financial Business Insights Powered by AI (Beta)
  • Financial Planning and Analysis: Service Profitability Review Booklet
  • Financial Planning and Analysis: Review Booklet: Updated Value Help, Additional Dialogs and Commenting for Review Booklet
  • Financial Planning and Analysis: Hotspots in Multidimensional Data Grid Apps
  • Accounting and Financial Close: SAP Fiori app ‘Manage Operating G/L Accounts’
  • Asset Accounting: AI-assisted Explanation of Fixed Asset Depreciation Keys (Beta)
  • Asset Accounting: SAP Fiori app ‘Post Asset Revaluation’ to Manually Readjust Asset Values
  • Asset Accounting: API for Asset-revaluation Postings
  • Group Reporting: SAP Group-level Green Ledger: Managing Greenhouse Gas Emissions for Group Reporting
  • Contract and Lease Management: Intercompany lease processing enablement in Contract and Lease Management
  • Event-Based Revenue Recognition: Support for imminent-loss handling for fixed-price customer projects and project-based sales
  • Event-Based Revenue Recognition: Shared-risk-based Revenue Recognition for Professional Service Projects
  • Cash Management: Optimization of Cash Flow Reporting by Including an Estimated Value of Default Bank Accounts
  • Advanced Payment Management: Migration Concept from In-house Cash to In-house Banking
  • Advanced Payment Management: Integration of Bank Account Management and In-house Banking Through Electronic Messaging
  • Accounts Receivables: Automated Fallback Processing Rules

Video 1: Release Highlight Video for Finance in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408

Here are the topics covered in the release video:

Financial Planning and Analysis: Cost Center Review Booklet

First off, we introduce the new Cost Center Review booklet, the second member of our highly acclaimed Review Booklet series. This tool allows overhead accountants to analyze and validate cost center data through predefined reports. It offers transparency in cost center reporting with business applications that combine application logic and comprehensive insights tabled in a user-friendly interface. You can effortlessly analyze and strategize actuals and plan efficiently for different scenarios.

With this app, you can display, analyze, and validate cost center data. The app displays information about actual and planned costs and groups several predefined reports under Actuals and Plan/Actuals pages.

Value Proposition

The cost centers review booklet helps overhead cost accountants achieve more transparency into cost center reporting by:

  • Providing business applications that embed insights into the relevant business context and help answer critical business questions for daily jobs out of the box.
  • Representing a new category of SAP Fiori apps that combine application logic and guidance with integrated multidimensional insights.
  • Helping to fulfill the promise of insight to action within embedded analytics.

Demo 1: Cost Center Review Booklet

Capabilities

  • Analysis of actuals and plan data in one report based on predefined pages to answer critical business questions efficiently.
  • Switch between guided views and generic slicing, dicing, and pivoting based on your business needs.

For more information, see Cost Centers Review Booklet

Financial Planning and Analysis: Review Booklet for Cost Centers: Gaining Financial Business Insights Powered by AI (Beta)

You can now gain financial business insights powered by AI. This feature employs a digital assistant to radically streamline your data analysis process. Whether it's analyzing tabular data or sharing results directly from our app, managing cost center data has never been easier. What's more - this feature is currently in our AI beta program, offering you a sneak peek into the exciting future expansions of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Value Proposition

This feature supports you, as an overhead accountant, with the following tasks:

  • Analyze cost center data with increased efficiency.
  • Understand and interpret data with the help of the digital assistant (powered by generative AI).

Demo 2: Gaining Financial Business Insight Powered by AI

Capabilities

  • Based on the Actuals or Plan/Actuals page you’ve selected, you can:
  • Analyze tabular data.
  • Summarize tabular data.
  • Edit, copy, export, and share the results directly from the app.

For more information, see: Get Involved Early. Register and experience the latest AI features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and influence upcoming AI developments. 

Financial Planning and Analysis: Service Profitability Review Booklet

We present the third member in the review booklet series: the Service Profitability Review Booklet for sales accountants. This booklet offers an in-depth, interactive glance at service-related financial Key Performance Indicators. You can now validate KPIs like billed revenue, recognized revenue, revenue adjustment, deferred revenue, accrued revenue among others under Margin Views - demystifying all dimensions of service profitability for service orders and service contracts. Easily stay ahead of your finances and ensure your service profitability stays right on target.

Value Proposition

You can analyze the following KPIs under Margin Views:

  • Revenue KPIs: Billed Revenue, Recognized Revenue, Revenue Adjustment, Deferred Revenue, Accrued Revenue.
  • Cost KPIs: Actual Costs, Recognized COS, COS Adjustment, Deferred COS, Accrued COS.
  • Margin KPIs: Recognized Margin.
  • Other KPIs: Reserves (You can monitor plan and actual comparison for P&L accounts).
  • KPIs: Actuals, Baseline, Ongoing.

Demo 3: Service Profitability Review Booklet

Capabilities

  • For Service Order you can report on margin views, actual view, and plan information.
  • For Service Contract you can report on margin views and actual view.
  • Line items will only be included in the calculation if the journal entry is assigned to a service (the service document is filled in the journal entry). Values are Shown in the company code currency.

For more information, see What's New Service Profitability Review Booklet

Additional innovations not covered in the video:

Financial Planning and Analysis: Review Booklet: Updated Value Help, Additional Dialogs and Commenting for Review Booklet

The Group Financial Statements Review Booklet has been enhanced with the following features: Active Page Filters button, Add Calculation function (see image), Conditional formatting dependent on threshold values in views and hierarchy in context menu.

Capabilities:

  • Active Page Filters button: It displays the number of active filters, and you use it to open the Page Filters panel in the side panel. This is where you add and remove page filters and their values.
  • Add Calculation: You can create calculations based on the measures or structures of your data source and add them to your table and chart.
  • Save conditional formatting in views: On the Formatting tab, you can define threshold values and display them with different background colors or as colored values. Now you can save such formatting as part of a private or public view.
  • Hierarchy in context menu: You can open the context menu of a hierarchy to drill to a lower level in the hierarchy or open all levels.

Figure 1: Add Calculation in Group Reporting Review Booklet

For more information, see: SAP Help Portal.

Financial Planning and Analysis: Hotspots in Multidimensional Data Grid Apps

As of release SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2408, you can see for which UI elements in-app help is available.

Value Proposition

  • So far, the in-app help was displayed in the help panel but you couldn't see the connection between the information and the UI. This has not been resolved.

Capabilities

  • How it works: Choose the question mark in the upper right corner to open the in-app help. The help panel on the right opens, and the little question marks (hotspots) tell you for which UI elements in-app help is available. To display the help, choose the question mark for the UI element you're interested in.

Figure 2: Hotspots on Help Topics in Multidimensional Data Grids

For more information, see: SAP Help Portal.

Accounting and Financial Close: SAP Fiori app “Manage Operating G/L Accounts

With the Manage Operating G/L Accounts app, you can perform actions to master data of operating G/L accounts depending on your role and the system that you access.

Value Proposition

  • View the master data of operating general ledger (G/L) accounts.
  • Create a new operating G/L account by copying an existing one, and adapt it according to your need​.
  • Efficiently activate a new operating G/L account in a company code by copying company code-specific settings from one source company code to one or multiple target company codes.
  • Enable mass change of attributes of multiple operating G/L accounts.

Capabilities

  • Streamline processes by focusing on operating G/L accounts.
  • Increase performance through a modern SAP Fiori app.
  • Smooth the user experience with a harmonized UI.

Figure 3: New Manage Operating Chart of Accounts App

For more information, see:  SAP Help Portal

Asset Accounting: AI-assisted Explanation of Fixed Asset Depreciation Keys (Beta)

Analyzing asset values for different depreciation areas can be difficult due to complex depreciation key settings. Powerful SAP Business AI helps you to easily understand the configuration settings of depreciation keys, resulting in efficiency and better decision-making for asset accounting and financial reporting.

Value Proposition

  • Reduce the time to onboard an asset by reducing the time to understand the complex setup of depreciation keys.
  • Improve business user productivity by easily understanding the asset and depreciation values.
  • Reduce the time to process periodic activities by reducing the time it takes to understand depreciation values. Periodic activities can be performed efficiently and in less time.

Demo 4: AI-assisted Explanation of Depreciation Key Configuration

Capabilities

  • Explains how depreciation keys operate and the rationale behind the system's depreciation calculations in a user-friendly and easy-to-understand natural language for business users.

More information: Customer Influence Page under SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, AI-assisted explanation of fixed asset depreciation keys in Finance.

Asset Accounting: SAP Fiori app ‘Post Asset Revaluation’ to Manually Readjust Asset Values

Use the SAP Fiori app Post Asset Revaluation (F7775) to manually post revaluations for assets and adjust asset values to either reflect inflation or apply market value principles. From the Manage Fixed Assets app (F3425), you can navigate to the SAP Fiori app Post Asset Revaluation (F7775) by choosing the Post menu button.

Value Proposition

  • Improved flexibility in asset revaluation postings, for example, by processing revaluation postings to offset an inflation-related cash devaluation or to apply market value principles in Asset Accounting.
  • Apply revaluations to acquisition and production costs (APC) and ordinary depreciation amounts.

Demo 5: New Post Asset Revaluation App

Capabilities

  • Post revaluation for an asset acquired either in the current fiscal year or in a previous fiscal year.
  • Revaluate an asset for selected ledgers and depreciation areas.
  • If necessary, adjust the specified revaluation amounts before the actual posting, by displaying the asset line items and choosing the Adjust Translated Values button.

More Information: Help Portal (Documentation) under Post Asset Revaluation.

Asset Accounting: API for Asset-revaluation Postings

Import asset revaluation postings into your SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition system using the API Fixed Asset – Post Asset Revaluation (API_FIXEDASSETREVALUATION_G4BA). You can use the OData version 4 service to post a one-time revaluation for one or more assets.

Value Proposition

  • Increase flexibility in asset revaluation postings.
  • Improve data accuracy compared to manually entering revaluation postings.
  • Reduce costs compared to manually entering revaluation postings.

Capabilities

  • Perform asset revaluations for multiple assets in one request.
  • Revaluate assets for selected ledgers and depreciation areas.
  • Apply revaluations to acquisition and production costs (APCs) and ordinary depreciation amounts.

More Information: Help Portal (Documentation) under Fixed Asset – Post Asset Revaluation.

Group Reporting: SAP Group-level Green Ledger: Managing Greenhouse Gas Emissions for Group Reporting

With this release, Group Reporting provides you with specific additional fields to support you in fulfilling mandatory quantitative disclosures for the emissions of greenhouse gas. After activating a business function, the following new fields are available to maintain the greenhouse gas emission quantity breakdowns in your SAP S/4HANA Cloud system:

  • GHG Scope
  • GHG Categor
  • Scope 2 Calculation Method​
  • Mode of Transport
  • Scope 2 Contractual Instrument

These fields are visible in the Define Consolidation Master Data Fields configuration step.

Value Proposition

The fields support you in becoming aware of your company's carbon emissions, understanding their financial implications and increasing efficiency in managing greenhouse gas emissions.

Figure 4: New Fields to Maintain GHG Emission Quantity Breakdown in Group Reporting

Capabilities

  • Define additional master data for greenhouse gas.
  • Import consolidation-related greenhouse gas entries into the consolidation system.
  • Define selections for restricting the data range for relevant greenhouse gas fields.
  • Upload reported greenhouse gas data for one or several periods.
  • Check the logs of the consolidation task for reclassification along the consolidation process for greenhouse gas data.
  • Make manual postings to adjust the reported greenhouse gas data.
  • Post multiple manual journal entries to adjust the reported greenhouse gas data.
  • Display line items of posting documents relevant for greenhouse gas data.
  • Display different views on consolidated greenhouse gas data.
  • Display, analyze, and validate greenhouse gas data.

For more information, see: Fields for Greenhouse Gas Emissions and Additional Fields for Greenhouse Gas.

Contract and Lease Management: Intercompany lease processing enablement in Contract and Lease Management

With this feature, you can manage real estate lease processes between two companies that belong to the same corporate group.

Value Proposition

  • Streamline the process of tracking and managing assets and their use across different divisions of a company, boost operational efficiency, and increase transparency.
  • Ensure optimal use of company assets by clearly defining the rights and responsibilities of each entity.

Figure 5: Intercompany Assignment of Usable Object

Capabilities

  • Manage intercompany lease-out contracts.
  • Manage intercompany lease-in contracts.
  • Make periodic postings for intercompany lease contracts (accounts receivable and accounts payable.
  • Run periodic valuation for intercompany lease contracts.

For more information, see: What’s New: Intercompany Lease Processes in Contract and Lease Management.

Event-Based Revenue Recognition: Support for imminent-loss handling for fixed-price customer projects and project-based sales

With this release, Event-Based Revenue Recognition provides a new option to automatically handle imminent loss for your projects. If you enter into a contract with a customer, you would expect the potential revenue of the contract to exceed the expected cost to generate profit. However, this might not always be the case. Per accounting standard, if the costs of a contract that the company expects to incur to fulfill the contract surpass the economic benefits it expects to receive from it, this contract is considered onerous. If a loss is expected in this type of contract, the entire loss should be recorded in the income statement.

Using the integration with customer projects, Event-Based Revenue Recognition assists in adhering to the accounting requirements for fixed-price projects with an expected loss including professional service projects and revenue-carrying enterprise projects.

Value Proposition

  • Enable automatic imminent-loss handling for fixed-price projects in project-based services and project-based sales.
  • Automatically identify a WBS billing element as onerous, if the planned costs are higher than the planned revenue.
  • Record a loss reserve for the expected loss in the income statement.
  • Adjust the loss reserve according to the cost-based percentage.

Figure 6: Record a Loss Reserve

Capabilities

  • Support customers in meeting international accounting regulations.

For more information, see: Loss Handling with Event-Based Revenue Recognition

Event-Based Revenue Recognition: Shared-risk-based Revenue Recognition for Professional Service Projects

With this release, the new revenue recognition key SPTMSR is available. Its purpose is to support the shared risk model for customer projects of the time and expenses contract type. In the shared risk model, the service provider agrees with the customer on an initial scope within which the customer can be billed at regular sales rates. The possible risk of having to provide services beyond the initially agreed scope is already foreseen at project creation. These additional services or costs shall be billed to the customer at a reduced sales rate. Billing at reduced sales rates for the same billing element can be achieved by creating a second work package assigned to the billing element with dedicated sales rates defined for the second work package. With this revenue recognition key, you can conduct revenue recognition for this scenario and calculate revenue recognition values for both work packages.​

Value Proposition

  • Focus on time and expense-based projects in which the customer agrees to share risk by being charged a reduced rate if additional work is needed to achieve the project scope.

Figure 7: Shared-risk-based EBRR Posting Scheme

Capabilities

  • Enable more precise revenue recognition for the described scenario, where a reduced rate is agreed upon if additional work is needed to achieve the scope. During revenue recognition, the reduced rate for additional work is considered not only once the additional work is done but also once it is known that additional work is needed.

Cash Management: Optimization of Cash Flow Reporting by Including an Estimated Value of Default Bank Accounts

Since SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2408, you can keep the liquidity item through the whole document chain when the cash flows are moved from the Treasury and Risk Management data source to the One Exposure data source.

Value Proposition

  • Empowering the cash manager to define the rules for estimating cash flow values for the default bank account without having the bank account information, such as cash flows from accounts receivable or accounts payable invoices.

Figure 8: AP Fiori app Short-Term Cash Positioning to view forecasted cash flows

Capabilities

  • Provide the cash manager with a complete view of cash flows including bank accounts Use the SAP Fiori app Short-Term Cash Positioning to view forecasted cash flows.

For more information, see: SAP Help Portal

Advanced Payment Management: Migration Concept from In-house Cash to In-house Banking

Setting up an in-house bank can lead to increased manual effort. Using an automation concept, master data should be created automatically.With edition 2408 there are two new migration object within migration cockpit available for in-house banking.

Value Proposition

  • Enable migration services to automate the creation of master data in in-house banking.

Demo 6: Master Data Migration from In-House Cash to In-House Banking

Capabilities

  • Provide a straightforward way to help customers move from in-house cash to in-house banking.
  • Provide migration object for in-house banking accounts and in-house banking conditions.

For more information, see: SAP Help Portal

Advanced Payment Management: Integration of Bank Account Management and In-house Banking Through Electronic Messaging

The new bank correspondence framework in the Manage Bank Accounts app allows users to efficiently manage their bank account-related attachments and electronic messages in a centralized location. With this feature, the bank account opening processing in Bank Account Management is now connected with In-House Banking to provide you with a more simplified and streamlined process of opening in-house bank accounts based on electronic messages of ISO20022 standard.

Value Proposition

  • Simplified integration between Bank Account Management and In-House Banking enables easy setup of individual in-house bank accounts, complementing the existing mass creation method via XML upload.
  • Streamlined and traceable process of opening in-house bank accounts through electronical messaging-enabled bank correspondence.
  • Enhanced transparency and traceability in bank related communications.
  • Easy access to important documents organized in one central place.

Demo 7: In-House Bank Account Opening via eBAM

Capabilities

  • Centralized management of all correspondence objects, including electronic messages and attachments, related to the account opening process.
  • Streamlined and traceable process of opening in-house bank accounts in Bank Account Management by integrating with In-House Banking.
  • Creation of new in-house bank accounts, sending requests to In-House Banking, receiving responses, and updating both the in-house bank account master data and the bank account master data.

For more information, see: Opening In-House Bank Accounts via Bank Correspondence and Managing Bank Correspondence​

Accounts Receivables: Automated Fallback Processing Rules

In the Manage Processing Rules – For Bank Statements and Manage Processing Rules – For Lockbox apps, you can create and automate processing rules that make bank statement processing and lockbox processing easier and smoother. Sometimes, bank statement items and lockbox items do not apply to any of the processing rules’ conditions in your system. In such a case, the system cannot process the items and you need to manually reprocess them.
To address this problem, we have created a special type of automated processing rules: “Fallback Rules”. As the name suggests, the system can now use automated fallback processing rules as a fallback. Fallback rules have a more simplified Conditions section where you can only enter conditions for company codes. This way, you can determine that items that could not get processed by other processing rules are processed in a certain way, according to the actions that you have defined in the fallback rule for the respective company code.

Value Proposition

  • Reduced number of unprocessed bank statement items and lockbox items.
  • Reduced manual effort in reprocessing items.
  • Easy to use and simple to create.

Figure 10: Automated Fallback Processing Rules

Capabilities

  • The Conditions section is already partly filled in, so you just need to enter the respective company codes.
  • You can immediately decide whether you want the fallback rule to carry out a one-step or two-step posting.
  • You can choose any of the four available action types: G/L Posting, AP/AR Posting, Clear in Sequence and Reprocess Items.
  • Fallback rules are immediately active as soon as you have created them.

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts. Follow us on @Sap and #S4HANA, and myself on LinkedIn and X.

Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 Early Release Series!

From July 8 to July 12, we hosted a series of 20 compelling live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find a xx-minute-long recording and a presentation for LoB/Industry/Topic as well as 19 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand–anywhere, anytime.

Find All of Our Assets

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. entry.title: Re: Sloc to sloc against the bin to bin transfer
entry.content:
Hello @saikumarkatla 

First, let me tell that your process doesn't look standard as you are using 999 WM movement type for some reason. The standard WM posting change process doesn't need 999 movements.

Let me explain assuming standard WM configuration. MM 325 posting is a transfer of blocked stock between storage locations within a single plant. 325 MM movement type is mapped to 309 WM movement type. When a 325 material document is posted in MM, WM creates two quants in TR-ZONE interim storage bin in 922 storage type as defined in the 309 WM movement type configuration:

The quants are:

  1. negative quant in 922 TR-ZONE for the source storage location
  2. positive quant in 922 TR-ZONE for the destination storage location

At the same time a PCN (posting change notice) is created for the material document. 

Then a transfer order with 309 WM movement type is created against the PCN. The transfer order has two items:

  1. to move source storage location stock from a WM physical storage bin to 922 TR-ZONE. That will zero the negative quant in 922 TR-ZONE.
  2. to move destination storage location stock from 922 TR-ZONE interim bin to a WM physical storage bin. Again, that will zero the positive quant in 922 TR-ZONE.

The transfer order can be created either manually or automatically. Automatic transfer order creation is possible if WM can find both source and destination physical bins with WM bin search strategies. It is also possible that source and destination physical bins are in fact a single bin, thus the transfer posting is executed without physical movement of WM stock.

As you see, there is no place for any 999 WM movements in the standard posting change process.

Why don't you adopt the standard process, instead of fiddling with unnecessary 999 movements?

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA FUNDS MANAGEMENT-Part 2 (what’s in it for private owned Customers)
entry.content:
Hi @marco_redaelli 

Activation of Budget AVC in customizing is per year so you can decide which year is active. 

Do you mean that, given a budget loaded only for year X, a PO with a line with due date in year X and another one in X+1, SAP will check if we have budget in year X, but not in X+1, correct? 

YES 

On the next year, by rebuilding the commitment as you mention, if the line on year X has not been completely received, will it still consume the next year budget, right?

No this will consume previous year budget unless you do the carryforward of open commitment and bring it to the current Year. When I wrote about commitment rebuilding I meant the commitment raised for the year without any budget, now time has passed, budget for new year has been inserted  and these commitment should be considered in new year budget consumption. 

 

Best 

GIanluca

entry.title: Re: Feature "reassign" inside the CPQ and inside the quot...
entry.content:
Reassign action is not an action that is available inside quotes.

 

It can only be visible on the Load Quotes page after enabling it in Workflow, and Users who land on the landing page from C4C to CPQ, cannot access the Load Existing Project/Quotation page (My Quotes page), so they’ll always land on the quote that they’ve opened in SAP Cloud for Customer.

 

Other than the quote, the only pages users have access to are User Page and Quote Layout under the Setup icon.

Additionally, administrator users also have access to the following pages: Setup, Log, and Developer Console.

 

All of this is explained in this help document:

https://help.sap.com/doc/f72edd8bc0ca43b5b9727fdbdaf73cfc/2405/en-US/f8edda558a4b4d1fb6ee6d515f4a5e30.pdf

 

 

And the C4C-CPQ integration doesn't support certain actions such as Place Order, Copy, Reassign and Delete.

The same is documented here in this help page: Configuration in SAP CPQ | SAP Help Portal.

 

If you would like for these actions to be visible inside the quote, then please raise an enhancement request on the Influence portal.

entry.title: Re: delivery indicator for completed Purchase Orders
entry.content:
Hi, 

Thanks for reaching out!

For scenario 1:  The delivery indicator should be set up automatically as " completed" is GR is fully booked. If there are discrepancies in the quantity received or GR is not fully booked, the delivery indicator will not be set up automatically and will need to be set up manually. If the GR is fully booked and the " Delivery Completed" indicator is not set, it could be due to a system bug which needs to be investigated with support team ( you can raise an incident for the same). 

For scenario 2:  if the inbound delivery quantity is entered as zero, it means that the goods receipt is not  delivered. This could prevent the ‘Delivery Completed’ indicator from being triggered. In this case, you should ensure that the inbound delivery quantity is correctly entered. If there’s a mistake at the company’s end in updating the system, it should be corrected to reflect the actual quantity of goods received. 

Kind Regards, 

Roxana

entry.title: Re: Activate Open Item Management for G/L Accounts
entry.content: Adding to the linked Help Portal document which explains the process: This is the intended system behavior. You make the changes in the Q or D system (depending on your configuration environment, that is, in the two-system landscape or in the three-system landscape). After that, you trigger these changes in the P or T system, respectively, meaning that the selections you have made in the Q or D system are implemented in these systems. You don't make any selections in these systems.

entry.title: App Aggregate Flows in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
App Aggregate Flows by Start Date is giving an error "No flows for aggregation under criteria specified. 0 flows aggregated".


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: App Aggregate Flows in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
The Start Date for Actual Flows is defined in the Configuration Activity / SSCUI "Define Start Date for One Exposure" (ID: 106354).

How the Application Aggregate Flow by Start Date works is it will aggregate all the Actual cash flows before the start date configured in the above-mentioned configuration activity.

 

With this App Aggregate Flow by Start Date, you can delete flows in One Exposure from Operations to simplify the blocking and deletion of personal data. You can delete flows with certainty level ACTUAL in One Exposure and substitute them with aggregation flows, which do not contain any person-related information.

 

For details on Aggregation of Actual Flows refer to the help.sap document

 

You can analyze the same with the App Cash Flow Analyzer.

  1. Provide Value Date = Start Date.
  2. Certainty Level = Actual.
  3. Check the actual cash flows before the date which will be aggregated.

 

Regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Outline Agreement
entry.content:
Hi, 

Thanks for reaching out!

To check the balance amount available in the Outline Agreement, you can use the ‘Outline Agreement List’ or ‘Contract List’ report in SAP. This report will show you the total value of the Outline Agreement, the value of Purchase Orders created against it, and the remaining balance. Here are the steps to access this report:

  1. Go to the SAP Easy Access menu.
  2. Navigate to Logistics -> Materials Management -> Purchasing -> Outline Agreement -> Contract -> List.
  3. Enter the necessary selection criteria such as Agreement Type, Document Number, Purchasing Organization, etc.
  4. Execute the report.

In the report, you can see the ‘Net Value’ column which shows the total value of the Outline Agreement, and the ‘PO Value’ column which shows the total value of Purchase Orders created against the Outline Agreement. The difference between these two values is the remaining balance in the Outline Agreement.

Kind Regards

Roxana

entry.title: SAP Ariba Procure to Order 2408 Release Key Innovations
entry.content:
Welcome to our 2408 Innovation blog, we are thrilled to share with you some exciting new features and improvements coming your way in the Procure to Order space. Let's dive into some of the key highlights that we hope will bring even more value to your procurement processes

SAP Ariba Shopping Mobile App - Purchase Requisition Approvals now available

With the role-based experience in the new SAP Ariba Shopping mobile app, approvers now have the convenience of easily finding requisitions in their to-do list, viewing line-item details, and then approving or rejecting them, all from within the mobile app. This eliminates the need to switch to the SAP Ariba web app for this task, offering a more streamlined and user-friendly experience.

  If you are eager for more information, you can browse this blog post on how to get ready for mobile shopping!

 

SAP Ariba Buying, guided buying capability - Copy Additional RFQ Information to Requisitions

This enhancement means that Guided buying can now copy more information from RFQ events to purchase requisitions, so users and suppliers have all the necessary information for ordered items or services.

This means that critical fields on guided sourcing event line items like RFQ ID, Comments, Start and End Date, and Attachments are now seamlessly transferred from RFQ to Requisition, providing enhanced visibility and compliance for both buyers and suppliers through the source to order process.

 

SAP Ariba Buying, guided buying capability - Support for OData V4 APIs in the 2NV and 3EN Integration Scenarios

This is a vital feature and enabler for our S/4HANA or SAP S/4 HANA Cloud integration customers, as It provides an adoption path for the current state requisition integrations as the version 2 oData API has been deprecated.

This feature is an enabler for the other integration related features we cover in this blog for Guided Buying Capability with SAP Ariba Buying (2NV) and Guided Buying for Central Procurement with SAP Ariba Buying (‏3EN‏).

For more technical information please see oData version 4 details, version comparison

 

SAP Ariba Buying, guided buying capability - Ability to classify Attachments as Internal or External Documents in Guided Buying with SAP S/4HANA

In the Guided Buying Capability with SAP Ariba Buying integration scenarios Buyers can now indicate whether line-item level attachments are made visible to suppliers.

This critical indicator is sent to S/4HANA or SAP S/4HANA Cloud to ensure that only those attachments flagged as external are shared with suppliers when a purchase order is distributed to the SAP Business Network. This enhances the experience for buyers and suppliers, bridging the current gap and providing enhanced control and clarity over document sharing.

 

SAP Ariba Buying, guided buying capability - Improved Shipping Information for Guided Buying Requisitions with SAP S/4HANA

Previously, only basic shipping information was sent to SAP S/4HANA in the 2NV and 3EN integration scenarios where a delivery address was not defined as a plant, leaving potential gaps in key delivery information.

With this improvement, additional shipping details such as phone numbers, fax numbers, and email addresses are now included, facilitating more comprehensive and detailed communication capabilities between business entities ultimately helping users receive items or services more quickly.

This capability is enabled by the oData version 4 API previously mentioned, where additional fields in delivery information can now be leveraged. More supporting information for this integration scenario can be found here

 

We hope you are excited about these impactful new features and enhancements in SAP Ariba 2408 release. We encourage you to explore and adopt where relevant to your Ariba landscape, to further optimize your procurement processes and drive more value for your organization.

Please check out the other blogs, and subscribe to the authors for latest updates as well as the following resources on our YouTube channels:

  • SAP Products and Services YouTube channel - https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLWV533hWWvDk7zRbhOsWQkF5BGWokhHYH
  • "Spend Management and Business Network" playlist - https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLWV533hWWvDk7zRbhOsWQkF5BGWokhHYH&si=zTlYobcledkWNQ2M

Thanks in advance for your assistance!

entry.title: Replace standard message in Fiori value help dialog
entry.content:
Hello Team,

I have a requirement where i have to replace standard error message with custom message which is displayed on value help dialog when there is no seached value is found .

i have used fragment to display the popup of value help

 

kindly share the solution.

<core:FragmentDefinition
    xmlns="sap.ui.comp.valuehelpdialog"
    xmlns:m="sap.m"
    xmlns:fb="sap.ui.comp.filterbar"
    xmlns:core="sap.ui.core"
>
    <ValueHelpDialog
        id="plantValueHelp"
        title="Plant"
        ok=".onValueHelpOkPressPlant"
        supportMultiselect="false"
        cancel=".onValueHelpCancelPressPlant"
        supportRanges="false"
        key="Werks"
        descriptionKey="Name1"
    >
        <filterBar>
            <fb:FilterBar
                id="plantFilter"
                advancedMode="false"
                useToolbar="false"
                search=".onFilterBarSearchPlant"
            >

                        id="plantFilterGroup"
                        groupName="__$INTERNAL$"
                        name="werks"
                        label="Plant"
                        visibleInFilterBar="true"
                    >
                        <fb:control>
                            <m:Input
                                id="plantInput"
                                name="Werks"
                                submit=".onPlantSearchSubmit"
                            />
                        </fb:control>

entry.title: No planned orders created with PIR for FG Materials with strategy Group 11.
entry.content:
Hello,

I have situation where we have FG Goods maintained all the required details in the MRP1/2/3/4 with Planning strategy as 11 in the material master level.

MRP Type - PD, Procurement type - E, Strategy group - 11, Consumption mode - 0, Bwd consumption per. - 0, Fwd consumption per. - 0, Mixed MRP - 2, Availability check - 01, Production Version Maintained.

As you suggested we checked OPPQ also , ID selection is "01" and also remaining settings are maintained.

Rescheduling horizon is "0".

I have checked the config settings MD20, MD21, OMDU and PPH_MDAB.

But at OPPR Settings for MRP Groups are maintained as below.

There is a Adjustment Period: PIRs=1

Adjustment: Planned Ind. Reqmts= 2 i.e.,Adjustment in the past

After creating the PIR's by selecting period as Month its not generating the planned orders.

Requirement Type is LSF. and Actie Version as GP

Does it creates only one Planned order for a month and after completion of this only second month planned order does it creates 

Does my Scheduling profile has any impact on this?

I wanted to know how does it work by considering current settings.

Regards

CK
